preamble: The shift from the voiceless uvular stop [q] to the voiced uvular stop [ɢ] is a process of voicing lenition. While [ɢ] is physiologically difficult to maintain as a distinct phoneme, this voicing occurs frequently in languages with back-consonant voicing systems.

examples.note: Realization of the historical voiceless uvular stop as a voiced stop in specific dialectal environments.

sources: Holes, C. (2004). Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties.
